programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Best of Series 1-5 : Jeremy Wade's voyages have taken him across the globe to waterways far and wide on an endless search for man-eating creatures that lurk beneath.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Best of Series 1-5 : Jeremy Wade's voyages have taken him across the globe to waterways far and wide on an endless search for man-eating creatures that lurk beneath.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Volcanic Island Terror : On a volcanic island in the Pacific, terror grips a fishing community when something in the water leaves a trail of bloody victims in its wake. The only clues they have to the attacker's identity are the large puncture wounds it left behind.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Amazon Assassins : Jeremy Wade encounters the Amazon's legendary arapaima, one of the largest freshwater fishes in the world.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Coral Reef Killer : On the tropical island of Sulawesi, Indonesia, a tourist snorkeler and several local fishermen have been killed and injured by a mysterious sea creature that impales its victims. Wade travels to the scene to investigate.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Chainsaw Predator : Angler Jeremy Wade heads to the Australian outback to try and catch the rare and fearsome freshwater sawfish, a shadowy predator said to be 18 feet long and known to attack boats.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Atomic Assassin : Extreme angler Jeremy Wade is on the trail of a monster that leads him to the site of the worst nuclear accident in history - Chernobyl.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Asian Slayer : When Jeremy Wade travels to the Himalayas to investigate river attacks, he learns that the suspected culprit is a sacred animal and is forewarned not to touch the creature.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Body Snatcher : Daring biologist and extreme fisherman Jeremy Wade travels to Guyana in South America in search of a terrifying river monster believed to be responsible for the mysterious disappearance of several people.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Piranha : Jeremy tracks down eyewitnesses and survivors of piranha attacks, uncovering the fear behind the man-eater.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Death Ray : Jeremy Wade visits Thailand in search of one of the world's most terrifying river fish, the Giant Freshwater Stingray. Armed with a venomous tail, it tests Jeremy to his limit.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Razorhead : Terrifying reports surface of a creature terrorising the Florida Keys. Jeremy Wade sets off to the Sunshine State to find out what is triggering these unusual attacks.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jungle Killer : In the jungles of South America, Jeremy tracks down a fish that has attacked a diver.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Face Ripper : Jeremy investigates the case of a Bolivian man who had his face ripped off by a ferocious river predator, leading to a showdown with an old adversary.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The Mutilator : Jeremy Wade is in Papua New Guinea to investigate a spate of bizarre deaths on the Sepik River. He finds himself on the trail of a creature said to devour certain male body parts.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Secrets at Sea : A behind-the-scenes episode where Jeremy reveals just what it takes to move river monsters into the open ocean and the crew opens up about their struggles on board.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Legend of Loch Ness : Jeremy Wade is on a mission to track down the famous Loch Ness monster. The journey takes him from the depths of the famous Scottish loch to the volcanoes of Iceland.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Alaskan Horror : Jeremy explores the legend of a lake monster in Alaska. Can he succeed where other hunters have failed and hook the beast, or will he find he is out of his depth?

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Pageant of the Masters : Mike participates in the annual Pageant of the Masters festival where he helps bring Leonardo da Vinci's painting "The Last Supper” to life.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Mennonite Chicken : Mike meets a French entrepreneur and chef who uses traditional Mennonite farming methods to produce a better tasting chicken.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Disaster City : Mike visits Disaster City to train with first responders in preparation for any sort of global catastrophe.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Whooping Crane : Mike visits a wild life and research institute with the some abnormal methods for saving endangered birds.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Goat Cheese : Mike meets a former hippie turned successful entrepreneur who is rethinking the dairy farm and making delicious goat cheese.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Kings of Neon : Mike meets an artist who is changing the look of his hometown of Austin, Texas with beautiful neon folk art.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Le Reve : Mike witnesses the back stage magic at one of the biggest shows in Las Vegas and meets the man responsible for it all.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Hair Museum + Dog Colorist : Mike visits a museum dedicated to hair and its charming curator. Then Mike meets a hairstylist who has found the perfect canvas is man’s best friend.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Mütter Museum : Mike visits a medical museum and meets the historical record keeper of the human body in all of its glory, suffering, pain, and splendor.

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It
programme.desc: Chad Pregracke : Mike meets the man who is pulling millions of pounds of trash from America’s waterways.

programme.title: The Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: Episode 1 : In this third season, a group of men and a group of women are abandoned on the opposite side of the same island. Neither knows the other is there. Will these two small groups survive - and what will happen when they meet?

programme.title: The Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: Episode 2 : The women catch sight of a man in the distance, and when they go to investigate the two groups finally meet. But as a vicious tropical storm rolls in, the thunder and lightning have a devastating effect on amputee and war veteran Hannah.

programme.title: The Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: Episode 3 : Marooned for ten days, the islanders now face starvation. They battle dangerous ocean currents to set fishing nets. Rob develops a tooth abscess, and endures primitive surgery on the beach. And flickers of romance begin.

programme.title: The Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: Episode 4 : Tempers reach fever pitch when Tilly turns on Erika. Dan leads an expedition for food around the rocky coastline. With the tide coming in he must make a difficult decision: do they turn back or risk their lives crossing one last perilous set of rocks?

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Land, Sea and the Tiki : It's sink or swim for Natasha as she tries to deliver two ultra-rare amphibious cars to the swamps of Louisiana. And out in a desert oasis, Tamara needs to transport a huge tiki hut bar to a tiki-fanatic.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Shipping Wars: Pilot : The transporters compete for some unique shipments going cross-country as a 4,000 lbs., 22-foot steel horse and an oversized 7-foot-long Venus Flytrap come up for bid.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Double Down and Bean Town : When shipments hit the auction block, the Bawcoms score a shipment for a 35-foot freshwater boat while Roy gets stuck transporting an old English phone booth after he tries to teach Jarrett a lesson.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Bullship : When livestock hits the uShip auction, Jennifer makes her move and wins a haul of two bucking bulls, while Marc gets a fragile experimental aircraft. When this experienced cowgirl picks up the bulls, she finds them meaner than she expected.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Cash for Tanks : After Marc battles it out with the other transporters, he wins a load of two Military tanks headed across the country and Roy lands a mint condition Model A being shipped from one collector to another.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Pain in the Caboose : When it comes to the biggest and baddest loads, Marc can't help but throw his hat in the ring. Fighting off Scott and Susie, he lands the job hauling a 1928 train caboose. When he arrives, Marc finds more of a remodel project than a shipment.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Camel in Tow : Jennifer uses her experience transporting livestock to win a camel shipment from Oklahoma to Vermont. Between getting lost, stopping to keep her cargo fed, and cleaning up the camel spit, Jennifer gets way behind schedule.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Tavern on the Greenbacks : Roy heads back into New York City when he outbids the other transporters for a job moving a catering food truck from the city's famous restaurant, Tavern on the Green.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Jennifer's Arch : Jennifer may have taken her livestock specialty too far when she tops the transporters in an auction to ship a number of small animals. Between the smell, the constant feedings, and the never-ending squeals, it may be the last run Jennifer makes.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Baja or Bust : Marc is headed to Mexico when he wins an auction to ship a $100,000 Baja 1000 Trophy Truck. Leaving from Colorado, Marc must protect his precious cargo, and make sure it arrives without delay in time for the start of the famous race.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: May the Ship Be With You : Roy lands the shipment of his dreams when he gets to haul some large one-of-a-kind props from his favorite sci-fi movie. But when he is cut off by another trucker on the highway, he's afraid it may have damaged these priceless movie artifacts.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Two Halves of the Same Willie : "Handyman" Roy gets in a heap of trouble after he sees a giant Willie Mays sculpture in half while "Cowgirl" Jennifer deals with a sobbing customer who is sad to see his prized possession go.

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Survival School
programme.desc: Episode 1 : The young explorers are taught to survive in the wild, where they must make their own shelter.

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Survival School
programme.desc: Episode 2 : On day two, the kids get a crash course in survival, from crossing dangerous terrain to learning how to use knives safely.

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Survival School
programme.desc: Episode 3 : It's day three of survival school and the competition between the girls and boys continues. Who will prevail?

programme.desc: The New Maddie? : The ALDC girls move forward after a rocky journey to Los Angeles; Cathy returns with a surprise choreographer; Abby invites Ashlee and her daughter Brynn to come dance at the ALDC and quickly praises Brynn as "the next Maddie."

programme.title: Dance Moms
programme.desc: Nia's Last Chance : The ALDC heads to Philadelphia with a risky non-lyrical routine; Abby gives Nia an ultimatum; frustrated with the lack of support from Melissa and Jill, Holly makes friends with two unlikely allies.

programme.title: Dance Moms
programme.desc: Abby's Trash, Cathy's Treasure : The media goes crazy for Maddie's second music video with Sia; Cathy assembles a team of dancers who have all worked with Abby and are ready to seek vengeance on the dance floor.

programme.title: Dance Moms
programme.desc: Mackenzie's Time to Shine : While Maddie is away, Mackenzie has the opportunity to prove herself; Abby decides to push the envelope with a sassy routine in a competition against Cathy's premium Candy Apples team.

programme.title: Dance Moms
programme.desc: Hollywood, Round Two : The ALDC returns to Los Angeles to compete; Abby decides to manage Kendall's new singing career; Nia and Kendall work to become pop stars while going head to head with solos; the girls give their all in the group dance.

programme.title: Dance Moms

programme.title: Dance Moms

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: To Love and to Cherish : Dog and Beth help Leland capture a fugitive when they should be making plans for their upcoming wedding; tragedy strikes the morning of the event.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Bounty Boot Camp : Dog's longtime friend and fellow bondsman, Jim, has a son who has just started in the family business; Dog takes Jim's son along for some training as he tracks down a potentially dangerous fugitive.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: Modern Marvels Essentials
programme.desc: Distilleries : Produced in Scotland and the American South, whiskey is popular, has a colorful history, and is one of the world's oldest drinks.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Military Movers : The United States Transportation Command, answering to the Department of Defense, runs military transport like an efficient private shipping and travel agency; from the Civil War to US Transcom, track the development of military logistics.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Presidential Movers : The vehicles that transport the President of the United States aren't your ordinary planes, trains, and automobiles: they are top-secret; and for your Average Joe, there's only two ways to find out what they're really like inside.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Toys : A look at five categories of boys' toys and what relationship they have had on the development of young minds; Super Soaker water gun; Lionel electric train; Erector Set; cap gun; Lincoln Logs; Matchbox Cars; G.I. Joe; LEGO bricks.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The Electric Light : Thomas Edison announces plans for the central lighting of New York before an electrical system exists or electric light is invented.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Adventure Machines : Adrenaline runs high when Adam Richman visits the factory where Yamaha makes ATVs, WaveRunners and more; an inside look at a company built to get people sky-high in paramotors; amusement park Diggerland USA; mech-suits; submarine built for two.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Weird Machines : Some machines built for sheer spectacle.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Nuclear Subs : Nuclear submarines carry more firepower than all bombs dropped in history; since the 1950s, these steel vessels became U.S. defense cornerstones, sparking Cold War underwater competition and creating engineering miracles for deterrence.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Nuclear Tech : Nuclear research ranges from well-known applications, such as bombs and reactors, to little-known uses in medicine, food preparation, and radiation detection. It's also spawned ancillary technologies to store nuclear waste and clean up accidents.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Most Dangerous : The most dangerous snakes, sports, weather events; America's most dangerous volcano; tornado hazards; base jumping.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Most Shocking : It strikes without warning and kills in an instant; we fear its might even as we use it to save a life; explore the electric shock in its numerous forms.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Most Dangerous : The most dangerous snakes, sports, weather events; America's most dangerous volcano; tornado hazards; base jumping.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Most Shocking : It strikes without warning and kills in an instant; we fear its might even as we use it to save a life; explore the electric shock in its numerous forms.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The Erie Canal : Begun in 1817, the Erie Canal was an engineering wonder of 363 miles of water highway linking the western frontier to the Atlantic seaboard; it took eight years to construct and thousands of hours of brutal labor.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The Panama Canal : The construction of the 51-mile canal that took ten years to build and employed over 40,000 workers, 6,000 of whom died of yellow fever, malaria, and other horrors; an earlier, nine-year attempt by the French ended in failure and cost 20,000 lives.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Alaskan Fishing : A large factory-fishing boat hunts down, catches and processes 300 tons of Alaskan pollock each day in the Bering Sea; the crew reveals the inner workings of one of the most sophisticated and complex vessels afloat.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The Alaskan Oil Pipeline : In 1973, a desperate America, starved by an OPEC embargo, began construction on an 800 mile lifeline for its oil hunger; study the technological triumph of the Alaskan oil pipeline built over mountains and tundra, where temperatures drop below zero.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Engineering Disasters of the '70s : The sinking of the Edmund Fitzgerald; the Buffalo Creek Dam disaster; the explosion of a supertanker in Los Angeles Harbor.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: '70s Tech : The 1970s were a decade of excess; dust off your mirror ball, put on your leisure suit, and rediscover the gadgets of the era; Play PONG with its inventor and learn how this simple game created a billion dollar empire.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Welcome to Area Fifty-Something : All is well at Area Fifty-Something with Team Monster, until a lone alien imprisons the President and makes a permanent home on base.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Danger Wears a Diaper/The Toy From Another World : The president's birthday is here! Dr. C has made him a gadget that will make him appear younger, but instead blasts him to infanthood!

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: The Bath Effect/The Fruit of All Evil : Susan makes B.O.B. think that baths cure crankiness - B.O.B. leaps into action, chasing after Coverton to cure his crankiness once and for all!

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Frenemy Mine/Maximum B.O.B. : B.O.B. is determined to have Coverton be his emergency buddy. Instead, Coverton chooses to manipulate B.O.B. at a time when he needs him the most.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: It Came...On a Field Trip/Educational Television : The Monsters find an alien in the woods doing an earth studies project, which has a lot of sensitive data that could be used for an invasion!

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Flipped Out/The Wormhole Has Turned : B.O.B. discovers a secret switch that General Monger tells him not to touch. Will it get rewired before the Big Blue Blob can get to flip it?

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: The Two Faces of Dr. Cockroach/The Thing With One Brain : Dr. C is split into two through his new teleportation machine. Can the Monsters reunite the wild and brainy roaches before it's too late?

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Night of the Living Dog/Attack of the Movie Night : Sqweep fashions a canine brainwave device to make B.O.B. the perfect pet. It works great on everyone on the base but B.O.B.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: 98 Pound Cockroach/When Nature Shrieks : Dr. C doubles his strength, but lessens his genius. Can the other Monsters save him before it's too late?

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Screaming Your Calls/The Time-Out That Wouldn't End : When Dr. C invents a "living" smart-phone, Coverton diabolically plans to use the smart-phone to hack the base's mainframe.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: It Got Out of Hand/The Sound of Fear : It's April Fool's Day at Area Fifty-Something as Susan tries to prevent the Monsters from destroying the base with a prank! Dr. C's "Ph.D" is in jeopardy as a former teacher reveals a missing music grade in his transcript.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Vornicarn : A new alien, Sta'abi, comes to Earth in search of a ferocious beast called Vornicarn. Soon, everyone is on a chase in order to save the planet from Vornicarn's terror!

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: The Sorry Syndrome/Speak Not the Q Word : Susan is in a bind after spilling food on Sta'abi and apologizing about it. Insulted, Sta'abi hunts Susan to feed her "fralnick" to Vornicarn.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: It Came From Channel 5/It Ruled with an Iron Fist : Strange secrets begin to unfold when Derek returns to expose our friends at Area Fifty Something. Monger is away for the weekend! This is great news for Team Monster and Team Alien until they're introduced to Rule-Bot.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Driven to Madness/The Beast From 20,000 Gallons : Link and Sta'abi duel for driving privileges of a new supercar. Sqweep wants to drive too though, and takes off on a supersonic joyride.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: The Sneezing Horror/Prisoner of the Dark Dimension : With everyone down with alien flu, Coverton tries to steal base secrets, but fails to account for a huge gelatinous hallucinating B.O.B. Vornicarn's bad behavior lands him in the Isolation Zone.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: I Predict Horror/Destroy Chickie D! : B.O.B. eats Dr. C's "probabilitator" and foresees Coverton with a crown, but soon Coverton discovers the painful truth about his future.

programme.title: Monsters vs. Aliens
programme.desc: Number Seven!/The Friend Who Wasn't There : Sqweep has to go "number seven". Team Monster fears the destruction it will cause and sets out to contain it! Link pursues Sta'abi using a pheromone spray of Sqweep's design that ends up having unforeseen consequences.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Total Bananarchy : Pig wishes to the President for his birthday, but causes mayhem. Banana recruits Shragger as his bodyguard. Goat builds a sculpture for Pig's birthday. Cricket flees to Mars for a more organized society.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Angry Old Raisin : Pig wreaks havoc at his elderly neighbor's house. Goat accidentally sends a bad email to the President. Banana has to perform in his Forest Ranger talent show. Cricket wants to impress Dr. Eggplant Face.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Cow Duck Avocado Mantis : Pig wants to be a famous belt in an action movie. Goat is discovered by a movie director. Banana has to replace his hero on a hit television show. Cricket thinks he can make a movie all by himself.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: It's Time to Slumber Party : Pig can't find anybody to have a slumber party with him. Goat joins the armed forces in order to get a VIP concert experience. Banana takes up news casting as a quick way to become famous. Cricket has to stop picking his nose.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Secret Origins: Special Edition : How did the gang become roommates? They each tell the origin story in the form of a high school rebel comedy, a space adventure, an old fairy tale, and whatever is in Banana's imagination.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Moby Woof : Pig competes to become the next Picklemart mascot. Banana asks the sun to go away so he can sleep. Cricket visits each of his friends' dreams to ask them for a gift. Goat meets the world's biggest dog at the circus.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Prince Mermeow Moves In : Prince Mermeow moves into the treehouse. He banishes Pig to the North Pole, forces Banana to go spend a night in the woods, makes Cricket go to a public pool, and tricks Goat into making her room better for him.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Gold Rush : Banana searches for gold with his friends. Pig uses dancing shoes on a dancing date. Goat struggles to cross a busy street. Cricket loses his butt after turning it into gold.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: The Mud Munchkins Must be Crazy : Pig needs to find a place where his shopping cart family is accepted. Cricket needs to go to the bathroom in a clean environment. Goat loses her thermos to mud munchkins. Banana makes real life imitate video games.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: King of TV : Pig and Angry Old Raisin try to get back the TV remote. Goat has to cross a road toll. Banana befriends a train full of hobos. Cricket takes his new robot out to dinner before using her to obtain the remote.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Heat Wave : Pig wants to have a pool party. Goat gets confused for a secret agent. Banana beats the heat by stealing an ice cream truck. Cricket gets trapped in a cave.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Charm School : Pig needs to learn some manners. Goat wants to protect her favorite city landmark. Banana becomes a world-famous stuntman. Cricket wants to get Pig back to his old ways.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Road Trippin' : On a road trip to go see the Pretty Panda at the zoo, Goat stops at a concert, Pig stops at a trampoline theme park, Banana has to do jail time, and Cricket has to get him back.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Love Boat : The gang takes a cruise vacation. Cricket shows off his discount ticket. Pig wants more room in his body to eat pickles. Banana plays matchmaker. Goat has to cover for her mistake.

programme.title: Pig Goat Banana Cricket
programme.desc: Hotel It on the Mountain : The roommates open a hotel. Pig is the bellboy, Goat is the chef, Banana is the hotel manager, and Cricket is the maid.

programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Sabotage : The artists go head-to-head in a test of technical application, and the losers fight for their survival in an elimination tattoo challenge dictated by the victors.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Pins & No Needles : The artists think outside the box as they show their understanding of color theory, then get a crash course in new school style from their coaches Jime Litwalk and Kelly Doty.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Drawing Alliances : Composition must be perfect as the remaining competitors draw freehand tattoos on their canvases, and coaches James Vaughn and Megan Jean Morris offer the artists advice.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: By Accident or By Design : Every decision counts as the artists choose someone else's tattoo design and make it their own in a true test of adaptability.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Roll of the Dice : As the women's team plots to dismantle the men's team, the judges unveil a combination of detail design and cover-up challenges that are helmed by two domineering coaches.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Put Your Ink Where Your Mouth Is : With only seven competitors remaining, the canvases hold all the power as the artists are challenged to out-market their rivals and work outside their creative comfort zones.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Moving Pictures : The artists paint custom wheelchair spoke guards in the flash challenge and show finesse as they take on difficult back-of-the-neck tattoos in the elimination tattoo.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Unfriendly Fire : The final five artists are tasked with creating murals using paintball guns, then must work across disciplines in a traditional illustrative portrait elimination challenge.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Step Up or Shut Up : To earn a spot in the finale, the final four artists endure a grueling, blind-judged tattoo marathon and then endeavor to reinterpret a rival's best work.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Battle of the Sexes Finale : Seven coaches compete in the Clash of the Coaches Face-Off, the final three artists unveil their 35-hour back pieces, and one artist wins $100,000 and the Ink Master title.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Ink Master There Can Only Be One : Working solo to test their precision, the artists must create mural designs using only staples, then tattoo portraits of senior citizens.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Ink Master Compose Yourself : The artists create one-of-a-kind designs on protective headgear in the Flash Challenge, and composition is key for a daunting elimination tattoo featuring Japanese cranes.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Ink Master Race to the Finish : Five artists must tattoo a subject and style chosen by their competitors in an elimination challenge, followed by a 13-hour tattooing marathon to send a second artist home.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Fresh Meat : With a chance to win $100,000 and the title of Ink Master, 10 of the world's best tattoo artists put their skills to the test.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Botched Head Tattoo : Heather challenges the judges' critiques, a head tattoo becomes a medical emergency, and Ton Jones joins the panel as a guest judge.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Pasties and a Cameltoe : The artists test their shading skills on naked female canvases during the Flash Challenge, Al rubs the other artists the wrong way, and one more artist is sent home.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Ink Disaster Piece : The artists test their technical precision skills, a photo realism tattoo turns disastrous for one artist, and Inked Magazine's Todd Weinberger serves as a guest judge.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Game On : The artists test their color skills designing pieces for canvases with prosthetic limbs, and acclaimed tattoo artist Scott Campbell joins the panel as a guest judge.

programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Tears for Beers : A bar and a marriage are on the line as Jon helps owners Tim and Sara save The Brixton, which is rapidly driving the couple into debt.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Empty Pockets : When tasked with rescuing an immigrant-owned pool hall in Denver, Jon finds the biggest obstacle is the owner's pride.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Jon T, He Don't Like It : Jon must help the owner of The Kasbah in Aurora, CO, find a less rowdy clientele if he's going to save the once-thriving urban hot spot.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: In a Pinch : Jon is brought in to help save a bar whose controversial lobster-tank claw-machine game drew protests from animal rights activists, which in turn drove away customers.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Karaoke Katastrophe : A hostile chef and a lascivious owner complicate Jon's efforts to save Dimples, America's oldest karaoke bar.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: A Horse Walks Into a Bar : Owners Scott and Donna face foreclosure if Jon is unable to rescue Kid Chilleen's Badass BBQ by evicting the bar's equine and biker visitors.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Play. Some. Janet. Jackson! : Jon must correct the behavior of a drunk and verbally abusive owner if he's going to save the historic Gipsy, Las Vegas's first gay nightclub.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Beer and Loathing in Las Vegas : Chefs and best friends Christ and Mitch decided to open The Hammer in Las Vegas to cater to locals, but then a case of pneumonia caused everything to fall apart.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: There's No Crying in the Bar Business : Jon must restore trust between owners and curb rampant theft among employees to save Barlow's, a failing Georgia sports bar.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Characters Assassination : Jon must help a Yankee family learn to do business in the South if he hopes to save the poorly themed Characters Quarters in North Carolina.

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Don't Judge a Booze by Its Bottle : Jon faces a real challenge with Cashmere Nightclub in North Carolina, where the wild manager thinks it's OK to sell cheap booze from pricey bottles.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Granny Retires : Granny decides to take her share of the Clampett millions and move back home.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: The Clampett Curse : Jed and Granny decide to donate the burden of their millions to some college students and move back home.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: The Indians Are Coming : When Crowfoot Indians come to Beverly Hills to discuss a boundary dispute at the Clampett oil field, Granny prepares for war.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Elly Comes Out : After reading the Society page, Granny decides the best way to get Elly a husband is to throw her a coming out party, complete with possum burgers and gopher gravy. Mrs. Drysdale, hoping to forever humiliate them, is all too happy to help.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: The Gypsy's Warning : The Clampketts come across Narda and Yerko, two gypsies, who decide to run a scam on the unsuspecting clan. Involving both a marriage prophecy and a curse, the crooks see the families' naivety to their advantage.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: His Royal Highness : The Clampetts are visited by a real king, Alexander, so Granny and Mrs. Drysdale start matchmaking. The broke royal needs a wealthy bride and courts Ellie. Drysdale niece Doreen is tricked by a costumed Jethro and ends up unimpressed.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Super Hawg : The Clampetts acquire a hippopotamus when an advertising scheme originated by banker Drysdale backfires.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: The Little Monster : Banker Milburn 'Drysdale's precocious 11-year-old nephew swindles the hillbillies of all the valuable art objects in their mansion.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jed Inherits A Castle : Jed Clampett has inherited a castle in the English countryside. Jethro insists that everyone, except Granny, wear the royal raiment's of Elizabeth (the First, of course).

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Clampetts In London : The Clampetts head to England to visit the castle Jed inherited. Misunderstandings abound, from Jethro mistaking San Francisco for London, to the impounding of Granny's medical supplies, to the real intentions of the elderly chemist.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Clampett Castle : The Clampetts finally make it to their new castle and try to adjust to the English way of castle life, at the least the way Jethro thinks it is, based on his understanding of English myths and legends.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Greetings From The President : When Jethro gets his draft notice, Jed decides he should pay what it costs the Army to train inductees. When Mr. Drysdale hears this, he starts figuring how he can get Jethro out on a Section 8.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Army Game : Jethro reports to the induction center after getting his draft notice. The Army isn't quite sure what to make of Jethro.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Plot For Granny : Since Granny can't plant crops in their yard, Jed thinks the solution is to buy her a plot a land somewhere else. He figures a place called Happy Acres is the best spot to buy land, not realizing it's a cemetery.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro's Military Career : Jethro thinks he wants to be a naval frogman, but Granny is concerned her tonic has transformed him. Mr. Drysdale, concerned about finances, has Miss Jane distract Jethro with comics, causing him to want to fly to the moon.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Reserve Program : Jethro heads down to the Reserves HQ while wearing an ancestor's Confederate Army uniform. The Sergeant thinks he's an actor there to participate in a Civil War reenactment. Jethro runs home to tell Granny the Civil War is still on.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro In The Reserve : Col. Blake asks the actor playing Gen. Grant to play along with Granny so that the Civil War movie can continue. Meanwhile, Jethro uses his 6th grade education to take the written test to enter the Reserves.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro In The Reserve : Col. Blake asks the actor playing Gen. Grant to play along with Granny so that the Civil War movie can continue. Meanwhile, Jethro uses his 6th grade education to take the written test to enter the Reserves.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Cimarron Drip : Jethro believes his moment for Hollywood stardom has arrived. Casting for Bachelor Sheriff Knows Best is occurring, and Jethro mistakenly thinks he has been chosen.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Corn Pone Picassos : Granny competes against Mrs. Drysdale as artists and tells Jed about her uncle, the barn painter. Jed and Jethro go to an upscale gallery to buy a painting, but are mistaken for gardeners. Bessie the chimp dabbles with a paintbrush.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Clampetts Play Cupid : After Ellie seems to be no longer interested in Dash Riprock Granny decides it's time to do a little matchmaking. She thinks the handsome actor would be perfect for Miss Jane.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Housekeeper : Mr. Drysdale talks the Clampetts into hiring a housekeeper. Unfortunately, the housekeeper that is hired is uninterested in doing housework.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Social Climbers : Granny's old friend, the widow Adaline Ashley, comes to Beverly Hills, and Granny sets to playing matchmaker for her and Jed. Mrs. Drysdale and her snooty friends are misled to believe Adaline is a socialite jet-setter.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Diner : Jethro decides to turn his favorite thing, eating, into a job by getting the family involved in buying a diner. The family's choice of offerings lead to a lack of customers, so Mr. Drysdale tries to fix things.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Topless Anyone : Jethro, worried that his diner the Hungry Gizzard has no customers, has a wonderful idea. He busts in on Mr. Drysdale trying to impress the wealthy Vanderpools, announcing the restaurant is going topless. Confusion ensues.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: Ghost Hunters

programme.title: Ghost Hunters
programme.desc: Century of Haunting : On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS travels to Oxford, Massachusetts to help a family in need. The house has been in the Voas family for two generations, and lots of activity has been reported, from apparitions to shadow figures.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters
programme.desc: French Quarter Phantoms : On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS heads to America's most haunted city, New Orleans, to investigate the Old U.S. Mint.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters

programme.title: Ghost Hunters

programme.title: Ghost Hunters

programme.title: Ghost Hunters

programme.desc: Charlie & The Slumpbuster : The new "evolved" Charlie has to deal with a ghost from his old carousing; baseball-playing days when a unattractive woman he slept with just to break a batting slump shows up as a new patient in group therapy.

programme.desc: Charlie Tries Sleep Deprivation : Charlie challenges his group to do thirty-six hours of sleep deprivation therapy so that their "inhibitions come down and the truth comes out" - except the result turns out to be the exact opposite.

programme.title: Send in the Dogs

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: Fibre Trails and Diamonds : A team of expert witnesses follow a trail of fibers and DNA to solve three cold cases in Pembrokeshire; a fraudulent diamond investment scheme is exposed by a gemologist.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: Pollen in a Field and Fibres in a Car Crash : A body left in a field prompts the examination of pollen, establishing a new forensic discipline; fibers fused with car wreckage prove who caused a fatal traffic collision.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: DNA, Fibres and Bones : When bones are discovered on moorland in West Yorkshire, detectives aren't even certain they are human; a team of expert witnesses is able to reveal the victim's story hidden within the bones and bring two killers to justice.

programme.title: Evidence of Murder

programme.title: Evidence of Murder

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill

programme.desc: Brown Betty : While Walter deals with some very upsetting news — he tells Olivia's niece, Ella (LILY PILBLAD), a fairy tale that includes musical performances by Olivia and Agent Broyles.

programme.desc: Spookyfish : When Sharon Marsh's Aunt Flo makes a monthly visit, she brings a mysterious pet fish for Stan.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Merry Christmas Charlie Manson! : Dinner with Cartman's family takes a bizarre twist when Uncle Howard shows up after breaking out of prison with the help of his cellmate, Charlie Manson.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Timmy 2000 : When Timmy is diagnosed with Attention Deficit Disorder, it triggers a wave of prescription drug abuse at South Park Elementary.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Terrance and Phillip: Behind the Blow : When the boys discover Terrance and Philip have called it quits, they'll go to any lengths to reunite the duo and recruit them for South Park's Earth Day festivities.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The New Terrance and Phillip Movie Trailer : All the kids in South Park are glued to the TV, wild with anticipation to see the first trailer for the latest Terrance and Phillip movie.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The Simpsons Already Did It : Professor Chaos is frustrated when he realizes all of his diabolical plans to wreak havoc on South Park have already been done by "The Simpsons."

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: It's Christmas in Canada : The Brovlofski family is dealt a devastating blow when Ike's Canadian birth parents show up unexpectedly. When the townspeople decide to forego Christmas gifts and take up a collection to get Ike home to South Park, the boys are distraught.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Canada On Strike : The head of the World Canadian Bureau leads the country into a long and painful strike and the responsibility of brokering a settlement rests with the boys.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Crème Fraiche : Randy’s obsession with the Food Network forces Sharon to explore a new interest of her own.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Royal Pudding : The Prince of Canada is about to take a Princess and Ike is obsessed with the Royal Wedding.

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: SUPER HARD PCness : It’s never been more challenging to be a PC Principal.

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation

programme.title: Best of Dr. Phil
programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Family Drama : Family squabbles are hard to avoid -- especially during the holidays -- but what happens when that behavior escalates to cursing, yelling, and even physical attacks? Dr. Phil sits down with a family on the brink of falling apart.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Honeymoon Hangover : Are you newlyweds suffering Honeymoon Hangover? The Bahamas vacation is over, the dress is put away and couples realize they now have this to face: Married Life. Dr. Phil discovers how little couples know about each other before they walk down the aisle.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: For Better Or Worse : Some couples vow "for better or worse." With a 50% divorce rate in America, in the midst of marital crises ranging from separation to unfaithfulness, Dr. Phil helps manage rocky marriages. Dr.Phil tells how to survive infidelity or when to call it quits.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Marriage Crisis: Drowning In Debt : Money is the number one reason why couples say they get divorced. The country's current financial crisis has resulted in more couples getting buried in a landslide of debt which takes its toll on a marriage. Is your marriage on the rocks because of debt?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Make It Or Break It : Tammy and Paul wed for eight years until their marriage ended. After nine months apart the couple secretly remarried, unbeknownst to their families, who would not approve. Weeks after saying "I do" a second time, the two are having doubts of their own.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Affairs Of The Heart : Can married people be friends with someone of the opposite sex without sex getting in the way? At what point does a friendly relationship become an affair of the heart or emotional infidelity? Meet Randy, who says his wife, Amanda, has crossed that line.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Risky Teen Behavior : How well do you know your teenager? Could your child be participating in risky or illegal activities right under your own roof?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: The Love Triangle : A recent study shows that the number of men and women having extramarital affairs is on the rise. Could your spouse be cheating on you? Dr. Phil's guests are struggling with the aftermath of infidelity.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A Husband's Double Life : Are you ignoring warning signs in your relationship? Is there something concerning you that you haven't addressed? Dr. Phil warns his viewers: Don't think your problems will go away once you walk down the aisle, face the issues now before it's too late!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Forced To Be A Father : Dr. Phil's guests engage in a heated debate about the rights of men and women when a child is conceived out of wedlock. Should the biological father be forced to pay child support for a child he didn't know existed or did not plan?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: In Cold Blood Pt. 1 : A sleepy Indiana town awoke one winter morning in 1992 to news the lifeless body of 12-year-old Shanda Sharer was discovered in an open field. She had been stabbed, strangled, beaten, and burned alive. Who committed this vicious and heinous crime? Part 1

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Accused Of The Unthinkable Pt. 2 : Dr. Phil continues his work with a family ripped apart by accusations of child molestation. Father Jason agrees to take a polygraph test to prove his innocence. Tune in to find out the results and what's next for this family!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Big-Time Moochers : Dr. Phil’s guests say they have a moocher in their midst, but they don’t know how to kick this freeloader to the curb! Karen and Rex say they’re tired of their 24-year-old twins, Andrew and Justin, living under their roof.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Dirt, Lies And The Internet : Dr. Phil investigates a popular new website that dishes the dirt on non-celebrities. On creator Nik Richie's controversial site, TheDirty.com, people anonymously submit photos & negative comments about anyone in their community, tabloid style.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Accused Of The Unthinkable Pt. 1 : In a gripping two-part series, Dr. Phil sits down with a family torn apart over accusations of the unthinkable. Melody says her 4-year-old granddaughter revealed that she was touched inappropriately by a man in a mask, and that the man was her daddy.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Ask Dr. Phil : Dr. Phil goes into his mailbag to answer viewers' popular questions! He has help from commedienne and author Teresa Strasser. When Teresa talks to moms on the street, find out their biggest parenting and pregnancy queries. Update on Ted Williams.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Bullies Beware Pt. 2 : The conversation heated when Dr. Phil gave self-professed bully Temica RoShawn a reality check about her aggressive behavior. Guests Ross & Lyndy say weeks of bullying culminated in their 15-yr-old son being sexually assaulted.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Parental Abductions Pt. 1 : Families are devastated when children go missing, but oftentimes it is a parental abduction. Fifteen years ago, John Clark came home from work to find his wife and three children gone, leaving only a cryptic note saying they went on vacation. Part 1

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's week three on `Love Island' and the islanders are soaking up the sunshine in their very own luxury villa. It just doesn't get better than this, but behind the flirting and romance, who's for real and who's just playing the game?

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The islanders are having the time of their lives as they flirt and frolic in the Spanish sunshine, but they all know that only one couple will have the chance of walking away with the 50,000 pound prize.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The islanders are having the time of their lives as they flirt and frolic in the Spanish sunshine, but they all know that only one couple will have the chance of walking away with the 50,000 pound prize.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's week three on `Love Island' and our gorgeous guys and girls are definitely making the most of their unforgettable summer in paradise. But while the sun keeps on shining, the twists and turns just keep on coming.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Things are getting hotter by the day on 'Love Island'. While the islanders are enjoying the summer of a lifetime, they all know there are plenty more surprises in store.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's been a turbulent third week in the villa and there are plenty more surprises in store for the islanders. It might look like paradise, but `Love Island' life is always a challenge.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Unseen Bits : It's that time again so strap yourselves in for another jam-packed hour of exclusive unseen moments and all the big stories from the villa. What a seven days it has been, featuring gossip, drama, fun, flirting and romance galore.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's week four in paradise as the islanders continue to enjoy their summer. There are plenty more surprises in store, and while they might have arrived dreaming of romance, that £50,000 prize means that not everyone may be in this for love.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The temperature is rising with every passing day. In the villa, one thing the islanders know by now is to expect the unexpected, and the surprises come thick and fast. It's impossible to tell who's really looking for love and who is playing the game.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's week four on Love Island and the islanders are enjoying a summer none of them will ever forget, and all those twists and turns are guaranteed to keep them on their toes. One thing's for sure, nobody wants to go home at this stage.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's the beginning of week six of the island adventure and, with the finish line moving into view, the islanders are all dreaming of getting their hands on the massive £50,000 prize. However, nobody ever knows what's around the next corner.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The islanders are enjoying a summer they'll never forget, but as ever more twists and turns are coming their way. With the finish line inching ever closer, and that cash prize at stake, the boys and girls are desperate to make it all the way.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: As week six continues the islanders are more determined than ever to make it all the way to the final day. With so much at stake, some are safely coupled, while others are in danger of being dumped from the Island.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's week six in paradise, and with £50,000 pounds at stake, every one of the islanders will do whatever it takes to make it all the way to the finish line. However, with so much at stake, no one knows who can actually be trusted.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The twists and turns keep coming in the villa. And as the contest for the £50,000 prize heats up, the islanders face a dilemma. They might be friends, they might be lovers, but they're also in a fight to the finish that nobody wants to lose.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It's almost the end of another week and the temperature is rising once more in the nation's favourite villa. The finish line is moving ever closer but nobody wants to leave paradise. With a huge cash prize for a couple, the stakes couldn't be higher.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Unseen Bits : Week six in paradise is over and now is the perfect time to catch up with all the best moments. As ever, we've got all the biggest stories, plus an absolute feast of fantastic action nobody's seen before.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Into the Breech : Harm and Mac agree to help guide young student "attorneys" at Iron Forge Naval Academy, a prep school for the US Naval Academy, as they conduct a mock trial.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Boomerang: Part II : Hostilities between Harm and Brumby escalate both in and out of the courtroom and it takes Chegwidden, with Brumby's CO's approval to get them to deal with their anger

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: People V. Gunny : Gunny is charged with gay-bashing following an off duty altercation outside a gay bar.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: The Bridge At Kang So Ri : Harm and Mac board a plane bound for Korea to investigate charges that GI's shot and killed civilians during the Korean War.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Drop Zone : Mac is prosecuting a SEAL jumpmaster charged with causing the death of one of his men during a parachute insertion exercise.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Overdue And Presumed Lost : A salvage company tries to claim rights to the wreckage of the USS Dolphin, a WW2 submarine that was lost the day before the attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Real Deal Seal : Medal of Honor Navy SEAL Lt. Curtis Rivers is court-martialed for punching a Congressional candidate who claims to be a Navy SEAL.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: A Separate Peace, Part I : RAdm. Boone has become nominated to serve as ComSixthFleet and to get his third star. Days before the Senate confirmation hearing, SecNav receives an anonymous hotline tip alleging war crimes in Vietnam by Boone; Harm and Mac investigate.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: The Colonel's Wife : Harm and Mac are sent to Panama to investigate the possibility that the wife of the Marine officer in charge of interdicting drugs from Columbia was smuggling drugs into the US.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Mishap : "Skates", Harm's RIO when he was aboard the USS Patrick Henry, was the Landing Safety Officer in charge of the deck when a Tomcat crashes while making an emergency landing.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Mixed Messages : An intruder shoots three Navy people, killing two. One of the dead is a friend of Harm, who protects his reputation. Webb, counter-espionage, and disinformation are involved; Bud solves it. Mac and Renee return to town; Mac leaves again.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Jag-a-thon : The JAG gang takes part in a JAGathon, a charity-benefit marathon, which provides opportunities for personal pride and individual competitiveness. Sturgis feels uncertain, and Harm defends a sailor for impersonating an officer.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: The Mission (Part I) : Harm and Mac go to sea during wartime operations. Harm flies the next mission, hits the target, and draws enemy fire; his aircraft sustains damage, but he safely returns. Bud runs into trouble at work, and Sturgis becomes closer to Bobbi.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Defending His Honor : A Navy judge stands accused of vehicular homicide. Mac and Sturgis prosecute; Harm defends. Harm discovers the real cause of the wreck. Webb continues to pursue a terrorist, and Loren continues to promote trouble between Bud and Harriet.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Enemy Below (Part II) : The clock is ticking down with every minute vital as the JAG team take the lead on the terrorist case.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Critical Condition (Part III) : Bud has lost his right lower leg due to a mine in Afghanistan; during emergency surgery he faces bad trouble. Sturgis returns to Washington, and Harm and Mac disobey an order from the admiral. Bud encounters an astounding change of course.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Killer Instinct : Louis Cellucci is having a rough time aboard the flight deck of the USS Benjamin Harrison. Moments after he walks away from another failed attempt at fixing an F-14, shouts of Man Overboard! are heard across the deck.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: To Walk on Wings : Congresswoman Latham and a colleague watch four marine jumpers jump into the ocean from an M22 Osprey. With two marines left to jump, the plane is struck by a swell in the ocean. Harm and Mac are instructed to investigate.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Golden Days : The NCIS team works with Hetty's former Vietnam War colleagues, A.J. Chegwidden, Sterling Bridges and Charles Langston, on a gold heist investigation.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Uncaged : When Sam's wife, Michelle, is taken from her home, the kidnappers demand that Sam's arch nemesis, Tahir Khaled, be released from prison in exchange for Michelle's whereabouts.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Unleashed : Sam Hanna goes rogue from the NCIS team in an attempt to stop Tahir Khaled from ever targeting his family again.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Party Crashers : When Hetty turns in retirement papers and disappears, Shay Mosley comes to Los Angeles to oversee the team and makes staffing changes; Sam insists that Callen find a new partner.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Se Murio El Payaso : When the daughter of a notorious counterfeiter arrives in Los Angeles, Sam goes under cover as a financier; Callen partners with Anna to track the family's latest scheme.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Assets : During the murder investigation of a Navy Lieutenant on leave in Los Angeles, the team uncovers classified surveillance briefs she smuggled into the city that may have been sold to a foreign buyer.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Plain Sight : When weapons worth $1 million are stolen, the team tracks the evidence to a charity event; Mosley gets herself added to the event's guest list, bringing Callen and Sam along as her security detail.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Mountebank : Sam goes under cover as a day trader after an investment banker who has ties to Russia is murdered; one of Callen's aliases is the victim of credit card theft.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Can I Get A Witness? : LAPD Detective Whiting calls in a favor when Deeks' former partner goes rogue.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: The Silo : Kensi is transported to a missile launch facility to help thwart a takeover of nuclear weapons.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Where Everybody Knows Your Name : NCIS and FBI investigate the case of a Marine that is murdered by nerve gas. Later, Deeks shares his dream of opening a bar.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Venganza : NCIS investigates the murder of a federal prison inmate who was the adopted daughter of a notorious counterfeiter.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Line In The Sand : Sam is shot during a firefight with the cartel; an interrogated suspect reveals new information on the whereabouts of Spencer Williams, the man who kidnapped Mosley's son.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Ninguna Salida : The NCIS team go all out to rescue a team member and kidnapped child in a daring mission on foreign soil.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: To Live and Die in Mexico : Gravely injured and unable to communicate with the team in the U.S., Callen, Sam and Kensi, with an unconscious Deeks in tow, search for a safe way home

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Superhuman : The prototype for a tactical assault suit is stolen from a Navy base and used in a Beverly Hills jewelry heist, so the team must determine who stole the suit and what their next target is.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: The Prince : Callen and Sam are assigned protection duty for Deputy Crown Prince Kamal after an unknown assassin targets his decoy upon his arrival in Los Angeles.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Hit List : A cartel hit list includes pictures of the NCIS team, as well as Mosley and her son's names.

programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Poke High (aka The Red Grange Story) : A young football player, named Matt, from Polk High where Kelly and Bud go to school is threatening two Bundy records. One held by Al for the most touchdowns in one season, and the other by Kelly for refusing her advances.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Camping Show (aka A Period Piece) : Steve, Marcy and the Bundys head out to Steve and Marcy's cabin in the woods, but the merriment is halted by Kelly's PMS. The men leave for a bit to go out fishing and return to all three women menstruating and hostile.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: A Dump of My Own : Al decides to build a spare bathroom when the upstairs toilet floods one too many times.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Her Cups Runneth Over : Al must go to a far-off lingerie outlet store to buy a discontinued brassiere for Peggy.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Bald and the Beautiful : Desperate Al and Steve put their heads together seeking solutions to impending baldness.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Gypsy Cried : After a birthday party fortune teller predicts luck for everyone else but doom for Marcy, she insists the Bundys fly with her on her business trip where she confronts her rude boss.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Requiem for a Dead Barber : After the death of his longtime barber, Al lets his hair down rather than tolerate a visit to a stylist or a salon... staffed by women. When Al backs down and goes to a salon, he gets a really bad hairstyle that makes him look like a woman.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: I'll See You in Court : In the infamous 'lost episode', Al and Peg learn they have been video-taped getting intimate at a sleazy motel. The same thing has also happened to Steve and Marcy. They decide to take the matter to court.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Eatin' Out : The Bundy's go out to a fancy restaurant to spend an inheritance check for $237 from a late uncle of Peggy's. But it becomes apparent that the fine dining in public is not a part of the dysfunctional Bundy lifestyle.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: My Mom, the Mom : Kelly invites Peggy to her school as part of a presentation for career day.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Can't Dance, Don't Ask Me (aka Kelly's Dance) : After Kelly gets in trouble at school, the only way she can get out of punishment if she joins the school's tap-dance club.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: A Three Job, No Income Family : Al finally convinces Peggy to contribute to the household income but feels emasculated when Peggy starts bringing home more money than Al from her cosmetics sales job. Even Al's new part-time job at Burger Trek can't put him back as the breadwinner.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Harder They Fall : While driving Peggy back from the video store, Steve gets cut off in traffic and Peggy gives the driver "the finger." The Bundys to come over to Steve’s house as protection when he's afraid of a violent reprisal after being followed home by the man.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The House That Peg Lost : After asking the Bundys to watch their house for the day, Marcy and Steve return to find a big hole where their house once stood.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Married…with Prom Queen - Pt 1 : Peggy decides to attend hers and Al's high school reunion. After spending $2,800 of Al’s hard-earned money, she makes a desperate effort to get herself elected prom queen.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Married…with Prom Queen: The Sequel - Pt 2 : Peggy stands to lose the crown to Connie Bender until Al finally decides to stand up to Connie's husband Jack. After Bud and Kelly crash the party, Peggy enlists their help to rig the election.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Dateless Amigo : After his two best friends find dates for themselves, Bud desperately seeks any girl that will date him. Meanwhile, Al comes up with a million-dollar invention for a shoe salesmen's convention.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Computer Show : The Bundys buy a computer, which serves no purpose other than a hat rack. But what nobody knows is that the computer can talk. But only to Al.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Life's a Beach : The Bundys spend a day at the beach.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Here's Lookin' at You, Kid : When a serial peeper is on the loose, Al makes the ultimate sacrifice to alleviate Peggy's despair about being the only one not fall victim. Bud must tutor Kelly so she can pass to the next grade.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Married…with Prom Queen: The Sequel - Pt 2 : Peggy stands to lose the crown to Connie Bender until Al finally decides to stand up to Connie's husband Jack. After Bud and Kelly crash the party, Peggy enlists their help to rig the election.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Dateless Amigo : After his two best friends find dates for themselves, Bud desperately seeks any girl that will date him. Meanwhile, Al comes up with a million-dollar invention for a shoe salesmen's convention.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: The Computer Show : The Bundys buy a computer, which serves no purpose other than a hat rack. But what nobody knows is that the computer can talk. But only to Al.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Life's a Beach : The Bundys spend a day at the beach.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Here's Lookin' at You, Kid : When a serial peeper is on the loose, Al makes the ultimate sacrifice to alleviate Peggy's despair about being the only one not fall victim. Bud must tutor Kelly so she can pass to the next grade.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Hot Off the Grill : On Labor Day, the gender roles are reversed when Al becomes the loafing one while he forces Peggy to prepare the backyard barbecue for his creation of the famous "Bundy Burgers."

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Dead Men Don't Do Aerobics : A TV aerobics instructor tries to get the Bundys in shape, but instead, he becomes sedentary and dies of a heart attack after eating junk food.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Buck Saves the Day : Kelly and Bud need to raise money for concert tickets for themselves, so Bud sells Al as a camping guide, and Kelly cheats at a poker game with Peggy's friends.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Tooth or Consequences : A toothache forces Al to see Marcy's dentist, a divorced man who devotes as much of his time to his work as he does to his young female dental assistant. At the same time, Al pressures Peggy to cook him just one square meal.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: He Ain't Much, But He's Mine : Peggy begins to suspect that Al is cheating because he seems to have a lot of different excuses on why he is coming home later than usual.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Fair Exchange : The Bundys' latest money-making scam is to host a foreign exchange student in their garage. But the French beauty proves to be so popular that she ends up stealing all of Kelly's boyfriends.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Desperately Seeking Miss October : When a Playboy playmate walks into Al's shoe store, he's eager to get home and look up her October 1987 issue. But Al is a broken man when he finds out that Peggy has sold his entire Playboy collection.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: 976-SHOE : Al decides to open a shoe emergency hot-line with help from Steve in the form of a $50,000 loan.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: Oh, What a Feeling : When the Dodge finally gives its death rattle, Al digs up his secret car-fund shoe box and trades the Dodge for a ride to the local dealership.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: At the Zoo : Newly unemployed Steve decides he doesn't really want to work, so he fritters away his days with the Bundys, visiting zoos and taking fishing trips.

programme.title: Married... With Children
programme.desc: It's a Bundyful Life - Pt 1 : Al has finally saved up a lot of cash to buy his family a ton of presents they want this year. Unfortunately, a mob of late customers in the shoe store prevents him from getting to the bank and retrieving the money he needs.

programme.title: Pluto Classic Movie Westerns

programme.title: Man With The Gun
programme.desc: Robert Mitchum portrays an Exterminator--in the American West. He will, for a price, eliminate any outlaws annoying the peace of any Western town. When his wife, in disgust, leaves him, he pursues her, only to encounter his greatest gunfighting challenge.

programme.title: Mad Dog Morgan
programme.desc: The true story of Irish outlaw Daniel Morgan; who is wanted; dead or alive; in Australia during the 1850s.

programme.title: Bells Of Capistrano
programme.desc: A feud between rodeo companies accelerates when both want Gene Autry, as a crowd-drawing added attraction. This film features Gene singing five hits, including "In Old Capistrano" and "Forgive Me."

programme.title: Navajo Joe
programme.desc: Burt Reynolds, Aldo Sambrell and Fernando Rey star in this lusty, gutsy action western about a fearless Indian with a relentless vendetta against the band of outlaws that killed his people. Directed by Spaghetti Western legend Sergio Corbucci.

programme.title: Blue Canadian Rockies
programme.desc: Gene Autry encounters mayhem on a dude ranch in the heart of timber country. When a Mountie is murdered, Gene and Pat Buttram must find the real culprit and bring peace to the Blue Canadian Rockies.

programme.title: Silver Canyon
programme.desc: Gene Autry and Pat Buttram are sent by the cavalry to bring in raiders who are interfering with supply lines. Gene traces them to a small Army outpost where the outlaws set out to ravage the town.

programme.title: Apache Country
programme.desc: Cavalry scouts Gene Autry & Pat Buttram are ordered to break up a ring which uses raids to cover up its activities. Carolina Cotton assists Gene and Pat, while seeking revenge against the gang leader.

programme.title: John Sturges' The Hallelujah Trail
programme.desc: Chaos reigns on the wide open plains as a host of stars, including Burt Lancaster and Lee Remick, have their eyes set on a shipment of whiskey and let loose in this wild comedy that spoofs nearly every convention of movie westerns.

programme.title: Return Of The Seven
programme.desc: Starring Yul Brynner and a stellar supporting cast that includes Robert Fuller, Warren Oates and Claude Akins, this classic western adventure follows the legendary Magnificent Seven as they thunder through Mexico to face the shootout of their lives!

programme.title: Last Of The Pony Riders
programme.desc: Gene Autry tries to set up a stage line to carry government mail. Schemers try to get the government assignment for their own stage line, and plan to discredit the Pony Express by causing accidents.

programme.title: The Conners
